key_points: Ratio is the comparison of two quantities of the same kind by division
Proportion states that two ratios are equal
If a/b = c/d, then a, b, c and d are said to be in proportion
In a proportion, the product of extremes equals the product of means
Direct proportion means when one quantity increases the other also increases
Inverse proportion means when one quantity increases the other decreases
Proportional reasoning helps in solving real-life problems involving scaling and comparison
examples: Direct proportion example: More workers → more work completed
Inverse proportion example: More workers → fewer days needed to complete work
formulas: Product of extremes = Product of means (a × d = b × c)
exam_tips: Always simplify ratios before comparing them
Use cross multiplication to check proportions
Identify whether the problem represents direct or inverse proportion
Write units clearly when comparing quantities
Carefully set up equations before solving proportional reasoning problems
